entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, the host plant affected by downy mildew

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: A widespread disease of sorghum caused by the pathogen Peronosclerospora sorghi

entities.name: PERONOSCLEROSPORA SORGHI
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes downy mildew in sorghum

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced, which is lost in systemically infected sorghum plants

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of wild and weedy sorghums to withstand downy mildew infection

entities.name: WILD SORGHUM
entities.description: Wild and weedy species of sorghum screened for sources of disease resistance

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating 308 wild and weedy sorghum accessions for resistance

entities.name: GREENHOUSE CONDITIONS
entities.description: The controlled environment where the screening was conducted

entities.name: ICRISAT ASIA CENTER
entities.description: The research facility where the screening was performed

entities.name: SPRAY-INOCULATION
entities.description: The technique of applying conidia to seedlings to induce infection

entities.name: IAC ISOLATE
entities.description: The specific isolate of the pathogen used for the inoculation process

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW INCIDENCE
entities.description: The recorded occurrence of the disease three weeks after inoculation
